semanticCore: Clothing, dressing, garment, shell, or covering
quranicRelevance: Feeding and clothing orphans/needy (Kiswah - 'aw kiswatuhum'), and covering bones with flesh (Kasawna - 'fakasawna al-'ithama').

keyDerivations.meaning: To clothe, dress, cover, envelop, or wrap

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَكَسَوْنَا الْعِظَامَ لَحْمًا (23:14): Then We clothed/covered the bones with flesh (biological embryology progression).
discardedNoise: The annual draping cover ceremony (Kiswah) of the Kaaba, which is post-canonical in detail.
summaryEn: The root represents covering with garments, describing the social duty of clothing (Kiswah) the poor, and the biological covering of embryonic bones with flesh.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الإلباس والكساء، واصفاً الإطعام والكسوة ككفارة اليمين وإغاثة الفقير (كسوتهم)، والكساء البيولوجي للعظام باللحم جنينياً.
rawText: أَكْسَى : see بَصَلٌ. تكسى 5 : see تقى. كَاسٍ Having clothing: see an ex. voce رُبَّ. كَسَوْتُهُ سَيْفًا 1 [I invested him with a sword]. (TA in art. غشو.) كِسَآءٌ [A garment]. See عَبَآءٌ, and حِلْسٌ and مُسَيَّحٌ. === ذُو كِسَآءٍ, as opposed to ذُوبُرْدٍ, † A poor man. (S, art. عج.) [The كساء was evidently a simple oblong piece of cloth; for الكِسَائِى is said to have been thus named because he wore a كساء while in the state of إِحْرَام; as is mentioned in the TA, art. كسو; but it seems to have been sometimes sewed in the manner of the عَبَآء, which see; and see also مُسَيَّحٌ, and بُرْدٌ.] It is [properly] not one of the garments which are cut and sewed [but is a single piece]: (Mgh, art. قطع:) [a wrapper, or wrapping garment, of a single piece]. === رَكِبَ كِسَآءَهُ He fell upon the back of his neck. (IAar, in TA, art. ردع.)
